# Colors

The color system defines brand, semantic meaning, and mode-aware behavior.

## Philosophy

Use semantic tokens in components so color mode can switch without component-level overrides.

## Palette

<Canvas>
  <Story of={ColorStories.Palette} />
</Canvas>

## Light/Dark Mapping

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Semantic token</th>
      <th>Light mode</th>
      <th>Dark mode</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr><td>background/page</td><td>global/white</td><td>brand/secondary-dark</td></tr>
    <tr><td>background/section</td><td>global/gray-light</td><td>brand/secondary</td></tr>
    <tr><td>text/default</td><td>global/black</td><td>global/white</td></tr>
    <tr><td>text/link</td><td>brand/primary</td><td>global/white</td></tr>
    <tr><td>text/hover</td><td>brand/secondary</td><td>brand/primary</td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<Canvas>
  <Story of={ColorStories.SemanticModes} />
</Canvas>

## Token Mapping

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Type</th>
      <th>Token examples</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr><td>Primitive</td><td><code>--global-black</code>, <code>--brand-primary</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Semantic</td><td><code>--background-page</code>, <code>--text-default</code>, <code>--text-link</code></td></tr>
    <tr><td>Aliases</td><td><code>--page-background</code>, <code>--section-background</code></td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Accessibility

- Verify text contrast in both modes.
- Do not rely on color alone for status meaning.
- Keep state feedback visible beyond color changes.
